ActionKind.description: How an intercepted MCP tool call is classified.

oneOf.description: Read-only — no side effects. Passed through without interception.
Produces a side effect — captured for human review.
Cannot be automatically classified — requires human review.

AgentIdentity.description: Identity of the agent that produced this PR package.

AlternativeConsidered.description: A structured alternative considered during a decision (v0.3.3).

AmendmentRecord.description:
Record of a human amendment to an artifact (v0.3.4).

Tracks who amended the artifact, when, and how — for audit trail purposes.

amended_by.description: Who performed the amendment (e.g., "human", reviewer name).

amendment_type.description: What kind of amendment was performed.

AmendmentType.description: The type of amendment applied to an artifact (v0.3.4).

oneOf.description: Artifact content replaced with a corrected file (--file).
A patch was applied to the artifact (--patch).
Artifact was removed from the draft (--drop).

A new artifact was inserted via --file on a URI not yet in the draft
(v0.17.0.12.11 — recovers files an agent described but never tracked).

ApplyProvenance.description: How a draft was applied — provenance for [`DraftStatus::Applied`] (v0.15.14.0).

oneOf.description: Triggered by an explicit `ta draft apply` CLI invocation.
Triggered by a background or agent task.
Triggered by an auto-merge hook.

ApprovalRecord.description:
Records a single reviewer's approval of a draft package (v0.14.2).

Multiple `ApprovalRecord`s accumulate in `DraftPackage::pending_approvals`
until the governance quorum is reached, at which point the draft transitions
to `DraftStatus::Approved`.

amendment.description: Amendment record if this artifact was amended after initial creation (v0.3.4).

comments.description:
Comment thread for this artifact (v0.3.0 — Review Sessions).
Comments from ReviewSession are merged here during draft finalization.

dependencies.description: Dependencies: other artifacts this one requires or is required by.

explanation_tiers.description: Three-tier explanation (summary, explanation, tags) from sidecar YAML (v0.2.3).

kind.description:
Semantic kind of the artifact (v0.14.15). When present, the renderer
uses kind-specific display logic (e.g. image artifacts suppress the
binary diff and show a human-readable frame/resolution summary).

rationale.description: Why this change was made (from agent's change_summary.json).

ArtifactDisposition.description:
Per-artifact review disposition.

Tracks the reviewer's decision on each individual artifact,
enabling selective approval (approve some, reject others, discuss the rest).

oneOf.description: Not yet reviewed.
Approved — will be applied.
Rejected — will not be applied.
Needs discussion before deciding.

ArtifactKind.description:
Semantic kind of an artifact produced by a connector or agent.

Stored on [`Artifact`] as an optional field. When absent, the artifact is
treated as a generic file. The `ta draft view` renderer uses this to pick
the appropriate display format (e.g. suppress binary diffs for images).

# Extensibility
Future connectors can add new variants here (e.g. `Audio`, `PointCloud`).
The `Image` and `Video` variants are intentionally generic — they are not
tied to Unreal Engine or any other specific connector.

[`Artifact`]: crate::draft_package::Artifact

oneOf.description:
A raster image artifact (PNG, EXR, JPEG, …).

Fields are optional — connectors fill in what they know. Width and
height are in pixels; `frame_index` is zero-based within a sequence.

frame_index.description: Zero-based frame index within a render sequence, if applicable.

height.description: Image height in pixels, if known.

width.description: Image width in pixels, if known.

oneOf.description:
A video artifact (MP4, MOV, WebM, …) produced by render pipelines.

Text diff is suppressed — `ta draft view` shows a metadata summary like
"Video: 1920×1080, 24fps, 6.2s, MP4" instead of binary content.

height.description: Frame height in pixels, if known.

width.description: Frame width in pixels, if known.

oneOf.description:
An opaque binary artifact (compiled output, archive, model weights, …).

Text diff is suppressed — `ta draft view` shows a hex summary or
`(binary file, N bytes)` instead.

byte_size.description: File size in bytes, if known.

oneOf.description:
A raw text artifact (generated script, config file, data file, …).

Full unified diff is rendered in `ta draft view`.

encoding.description: Character encoding, e.g. `"utf-8"`, `"latin-1"`. Defaults to UTF-8 if absent.

line_count.description: Number of lines in the file, if known.

oneOf.description:
A memory summary artifact produced by an analysis/learning goal run (v0.15.13.2).

Produced when `ta draft build` detects an empty overlay diff but finds memory
entries written by this goal run. The changeset holds a rendered summary of all
entries (key, scope, value) for human review.

On approve: entries remain in the store — they were written during the run.
On deny: entries are removed from the store using `entry_ids`.

entry_count.description: Number of memory entries created during this goal run.

entry_ids.description:
Entry IDs (as strings) used by `ta draft deny` to remove them from the store.

These are the `entry_id` (UUID) values from each `MemoryEntry`.

ChangeDependency.description:
A dependency relationship between artifacts.

Reported by the agent via .ta/change_summary.json, used by the
reviewer to understand which changes can be independently accepted/rejected.

target_uri.description: The resource_uri of the related artifact.

Changes.description:
The changes section: artifacts (local FS changes) + patch_sets (external changes)
+ pending_actions (intercepted MCP tool calls, v0.5.1).

pending_actions.description:
MCP tool calls intercepted for human review (v0.5.1).
State-changing external actions are captured here instead of being
executed immediately. Read-only calls pass through unintercepted.

Comment.description: A single comment in a thread.

commenter.description: Who wrote the comment (human reviewer, agent, etc.).

reasoning.description:
Structured reasoning for this review decision (v0.3.3).
Reviewer can explain *why* they approved/rejected, not just leave text.

CommentThread.description:
A thread of comments on an artifact.

Supports multi-party discussion: reviewer, agent, other reviewers, etc.

confidence.description: Optional agent confidence in this decision (0.0–1.0) (v0.14.7).

context.description:
What external need, feature, or constraint triggered this decision (v0.14.9.2).
Shown as the header line in collapsed state.

oneOf.description: This artifact requires the target (can't apply without it).
The target requires this artifact (target breaks if this is reverted).

DesignAlternative.description:
A design alternative considered during agent work (v0.9.5).

Agents report which options they evaluated and why they chose one over others.
Displayed under "Design Decisions" in `ta draft view`.

chosen.description: Whether this was the chosen approach.

option.description: The option that was considered (e.g., "Use a HashMap for lookup").

rationale.description: Why this option was chosen or rejected.

DraftStatus.description: Review status of a draft package (internal tracking, not in JSON schema).

applied_via.description:
How the draft was applied (v0.15.14.0). Defaults to `Manual` for
backward-compatible deserialization of older draft files.

oneOf.description: This draft has been superseded by a follow-up goal's draft.
This draft has been manually closed (abandoned, hand-merged, or obsolete).

ExplanationTiers.description:
Three-tier explanation for an artifact (v0.2.3).

Agents populate this via `.diff.explanation.yaml` sidecar files.
Enables tiered review: top (one-line) → medium (paragraph) → full (with diff).

explanation.description: Paragraph explaining what changed and why, dependencies affected.

related_artifacts.description: Related artifacts (URIs) that are connected to this change.

summary.description: One-line summary (e.g., "Refactored auth middleware to use JWT").

Goal.description: The high-level goal this PR package contributes to.

parent_goal_title.description:
Title of the root/parent goal this is a follow-up to (v0.13.0.1).
Preserved so draft view and apply can show the chain context even if
the parent goal record is no longer available.

IgnoredArtifact.description:
A gitignored artifact encountered during `ta draft apply --submit` (v0.13.17.5).

Classified as either known-safe (silently dropped) or unexpected (requires attention).

known_safe.description: Whether the path matched the known-safe drop list (e.g., .mcp.json).

path.description: Relative path of the artifact that was gitignored.

Iteration.description: Which iteration of the goal this package represents.

PatchAction.description: Action types for external patch sets.

PatchSet.description: A staged change to an external resource (Drive, Gmail, DB, etc.).

PendingAction.description:
An MCP tool call intercepted during agent execution, pending human review.

When an agent calls an external MCP tool (e.g., `gmail_send`, `slack_post`),
TA intercepts the call, records it here, and holds it for human approval.
Read-only calls (search, list, get) pass through immediately.

action_id.description: Unique identifier for this action instance.

disposition.description: Whether this action has been approved for replay.

intercepted_at.description: When the tool call was intercepted.

parameters.description: Serialized tool parameters as provided by the agent (credentials redacted).

target_uri.description: Resource this action targets (URI scheme, e.g., "mcp://gmail/send").

tool_name.description: The MCP tool name that was called (e.g., "gmail_send", "slack_post").

Plan.description: Execution plan included in the PR package.

PolicyDecisionRecord.description: A recorded policy decision relevant to this PR.

evaluation_steps.description: Evaluation steps the policy engine performed (v0.3.3).

grants_checked.description: Grants that were checked during evaluation (v0.3.3).

Provenance.description: Provenance information: where inputs came from.

ReviewReasoning.description:
Structured reasoning attached to a review comment (v0.3.3).

Enables compliance reporting: reviewers document *why* they approved or rejected,
what alternatives they considered, and what principles guided the decision.

applied_principles.description: Principles or policies that informed the decision.

rationale.description: The reviewer's rationale for their decision.

Risk.description: Risk assessment for the PR package.

Signatures.description: Cryptographic signatures for the PR package.

Summary.description: Human-readable summary of what changed and why.

alternatives_considered.description:
Design alternatives considered during this work (v0.9.5).
Populated by agents via the `alternatives` parameter on `ta_pr_build`.

SupervisorReview.description:
The result of an AI supervisor reviewing staged changes.
Embedded in `DraftPackage.supervisor_review`.

agent.description: Which supervisor produced this review ("builtin", "claude-code", "codex", etc.).

confidence.description:
Confidence (0.0-1.0) the supervisor had in this verdict, computed from
the verdict severity, scope check, and finding count — consumed by the
shared Decision gate (`ta-decision::decide`) alongside `risk_score`.

duration_secs.description: How long the supervisor took in seconds.

findings.description: Specific findings from the review (concerns, observations).

scope_ok.description: Whether changes stayed within the goal's declared scope.

verdict.description: Overall verdict: pass, warn, or block.

SupervisorVerdict.description: Verdict from the supervisor agent review.

oneOf.description: Changes are aligned with the goal and constitution.
Minor concerns but not blocking — shown in draft view with yellow.
Significant alignment or constitution violation — can block approval.

ValidationEntry.description: Result of one `required_checks` entry run after agent exit (v0.13.17).

stdout_tail.description: Last 20 lines of combined stdout+stderr output.

VcsTrackingInfo.description: VCS tracking information for post-apply lifecycle monitoring (v0.11.2.3).

branch.description: Branch name the changes were committed to.

commit_sha.description: Commit SHA of the applied changes.

last_checked.description: When VCS status was last checked/updated.

VerificationWarning.description: A warning from a pre-draft verification command failure (v0.10.8).

WorkspaceRef.description: Reference to the workspace where changes were staged.

description:
The Draft Package — a complete, reviewable milestone deliverable.

This is the central artifact of Trusted Autonomy. Every goal iteration
produces one of these for human review.

agent_decision_log.description:
Agent-authored decision log (v0.14.7).

Populated from `.ta-decisions.json` written by the agent during its run.
Records non-obvious implementation choices with alternatives and rationale.
Distinct from `plan.decision_log` which is extracted from `change_summary.json`.
Shown as the "Agent Decision Log" section in `ta draft view`.

baseline_artifacts.description:
Artifact URIs inherited from the parent draft (v0.14.3.5).

Populated when building a follow-up draft. Contains all `resource_uri` values
from the parent draft's artifact list at build time. During `ta draft apply`,
files in this list that are unchanged in staging (staging hash == source hash)
are skipped — they were already settled by the parent apply and staging just
has an older copy.

This prevents "follow-up staging drift": applying a follow-up draft from
staging that predates the parent commit would otherwise revert files
(PLAN.md, USAGE.md, shared source) that the parent apply had already updated.

display_id.description:
Human-friendly display ID derived from the goal ID (v0.10.11).
Format: `<goal-id-prefix>-NN` (e.g., `511e0465-01`).
Falls back to `package_id` short prefix for legacy drafts.

draft_seq.description:
Sequence number for this draft within its goal (v0.14.7.3).

First draft for a goal is 1, second is 2, etc. Combined with `goal_shortref`
to produce the `<shortref>/<seq>` display identifier (e.g., `2159d87e/1`).
Defaults to 0 for legacy drafts without this field.

goal_shortref.description:
Goal shortref inherited from the parent GoalRun (v0.14.7.3).

First 8 lowercase hex characters of `goal.goal_id`. Populated at `ta draft build`
time. Used to display drafts as `<goal_shortref>/<draft_seq>` across all CLI
surfaces (list, view, PR title, audit log). Allows `grep 2159d87e audit.jsonl`
to find all entries for a goal.

ignored_artifacts.description:
Gitignored artifacts encountered during apply --submit (v0.13.17.5).
Populated by the VCS adapter when `git add` would fail on ignored paths.
Known-safe artifacts (.mcp.json, *.local.toml, .ta/ runtime files) are
recorded here but silently dropped from the commit.
Unexpected-ignored artifacts are highlighted in `ta draft view`.

parent_draft_id.description:
Parent draft ID for follow-up goals (v0.12.2.1).
When set, this draft is a follow-up to the parent draft. Used for chain
display (`ta draft view` combined impact) and chain apply (`ta draft apply --chain`).

pending_approvals.description:
Accumulated reviewer approvals for multi-party governance (v0.14.2).
Empty for single-approver workflows (legacy / require_approvals = 1).
Grows as each reviewer calls `ta draft approve --as <identity>`.
When `pending_approvals.len() >= require_approvals` the draft transitions
to `DraftStatus::Approved`.

plan_md_base.description:
PLAN.md content at staging-creation time (v0.15.24.5).

Captured from `<staging>/.ta/plan_base.md` at `ta draft build` time.
Used during `ta draft apply` to perform a 3-way merge: agent additions
(base→staging diff) are applied on top of source (which may have accumulated
its own changes since the goal started). Preserves both the agent's new
phase sections AND source's item history that the agent never saw.

`None` for legacy drafts built before v0.15.24.5 — apply falls back to
keeping source unchanged and logging a warning.

plan_phase.description:
Plan phase ID linked to this draft (v0.15.15.2).

Populated from `GoalRun.plan_phase` at `ta draft build` time.
Shown prominently in `ta draft view` (below the draft title) and as
a column in `ta draft list`. Empty for goals not linked to a plan phase.

status.description: Tracks the review status (not in the JSON schema — internal state).

supervisor_review.description:
AI supervisor review embedded after agent exit (v0.13.17.4).
Present when supervisor is enabled; `None` when disabled or skipped.

tag.description:
Human-friendly goal tag inherited from the parent GoalRun (v0.11.2.3).
The primary display identifier in all draft listing contexts.

validation_log.description:
Hard evidence that required checks passed/failed (v0.13.17).
Each entry records the outcome of one required_check command.
Non-zero exit_code blocks `ta draft approve` unless --override is passed.

vcs_status.description:
VCS tracking information populated during commit/push/open_review (v0.11.2.3).
Tracks the PR lifecycle after apply.

verification_warnings.description:
Verification warnings from pre-draft verification gate (v0.10.8).
Populated when `[verify] on_failure = "warn"` and a command fails.

work_plan.description:
Planner work plan (v0.15.20).

Populated from `.ta/work-plan.json` when a `plan_work` stage preceded the
implementor. Shown as the "Implementation Plan" section in `ta draft view`
before the file diff, giving reviewers reasoning context before code.

title: DraftPackage
